# game — comprehensive LLM reference

> An ECS game framework for Moku — Spark-style public API and memory layout, PixiJS v8 rendering,
> built on @moku-labs/core, composable with @moku-labs/web, with a first-class MCP plugin exposing
> the live runtime to agent clients over stdio and/or Streamable HTTP.

This is the comprehensive reference for `game`. Everything below is grounded in source. The package
is **unpublished** (`name: "game"`, `version: "0.0.0"`). Use `bun` exclusively. Per-plugin READMEs
under `src/plugins/<name>/README.md` are the authoritative API source and remain the canonical
detail; this file consolidates the public surface.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
## 1. The three-layer Moku model (factory chain)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`game` follows the Moku Core three-layer model. Consumers only ever touch Layer 3.

**Layer 1 — `src/config.ts`** (config + events):

```ts
import { envPlugin, logPlugin } from "@moku-labs/common";
import { createCoreConfig } from "@moku-labs/core";

// The framework defines NO global config fields — all config is per-plugin.
export type Config = {};

// Coarse framework event contract. Hot-path frame work is NOT emitted as kernel events.
export type Events = {
  "assets:loaded": { alias: string; kind: "asset" | "bundle" };
  "scene:loaded": { name: string };
};

export const coreConfig = createCoreConfig<Config, Events, [typeof logPlugin, typeof envPlugin]>(
  "game",
  { config: {}, plugins: [logPlugin, envPlugin] } // → ctx.log + ctx.env on every plugin ctx
);

export const createPlugin = coreConfig.createPlugin;
export const createCore = coreConfig.createCore;
```

`logPlugin` and `envPlugin` from `@moku-labs/common` are registered here, so `ctx.log` (structured
logging) and `ctx.env` (validated environment access) are available on every plugin's context.

**Layer 2 — `src/index.ts`** (framework + plugins):

```ts
const framework = createCore(coreConfig, {
  plugins: [
    ecsPlugin, schedulerPlugin, rendererPlugin, inputPlugin,
    loopPlugin, assetsPlugin, contextPlugin, scenePlugin, mcpPlugin
  ],
  pluginConfigs: {
    context: { bindGameContext: true } // framework default; consumers override via createApp
  }
});

export * from "./plugins";            // plugin instances + Ecs/Scheduler/Context/... type namespaces
export const createApp = framework.createApp;
export const createPlugin = framework.createPlugin;
```

**Layer 3 — your app**:

```ts
import { createApp } from "game";

const app = createApp({ pluginConfigs: { /* per-plugin overrides */ } });
await app.start();   // async — boots Pixi Application, registers DOM listeners + systems, binds resources
// app.ecs, app.scheduler, app.renderer, app.input, app.loop, app.assets, app.context, app.scene, app.mcp
await app.stop();    // async — destroys GPU Application, removes listeners, closes MCP server
```

`createApp(...)` returns the App **synchronously**; `app.start()` is **async** and must be awaited
before using lifecycle-owning plugin APIs (renderer/input/loop/context/mcp). `app.ecs` and
`app.scheduler` are usable immediately after `start()` (the ECS world is built eagerly in
`createState`).

**Custom plugins** integrate via `createPlugin` from `game`:

```ts
import { createPlugin } from "game";
import { assetsPlugin } from "game"; // or "game/.../assets" — depend on the instance

const myPlugin = createPlugin("myPlugin", {
  depends: [assetsPlugin],                 // declares the dependency edge
  config: { speed: 1 },                    // per-plugin config (override via pluginConfigs.myPlugin)
  createState: () => ({ ticks: 0 }),       // mutable state object
  api: (ctx) => ({                          // exposed as app.myPlugin
    bump: () => { ctx.state.ticks++; }
  }),
  hooks: (_ctx) => ({                       // subscribe to framework events
    "assets:loaded": ({ alias, kind }) => { /* react */ }
  }),
  lifecycle: {                              // onStart/onStop for resource-owning plugins
    onStart: async (ctx) => {
      const assets = ctx.require(assetsPlugin); // resolve a dependency's API
      // ...
    }
  }
});
```

Resolve a dependency's API at runtime with `ctx.require(<pluginInstance>)`. `depends` declares the
edge (and ordering); `ctx.require` returns the typed API. Resolving lazily inside methods/lifecycle
(not at construction) guarantees the dependency has started.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
## 2. Plugin dependency graph & initialization order
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Registration order in `src/index.ts` matches the dependency topology:

```
ecs            (no deps — foundation, plugin #1)
 └─ scheduler  (depends: ecs)
     ├─ renderer (depends: ecs, scheduler)
     └─ input    (depends: scheduler)
         ├─ loop    (depends: scheduler, renderer, ecs)
         └─ assets  (depends: renderer)
             ├─ context (depends: ecs, assets)
             └─ scene   (depends: ecs, renderer, assets)
                 └─ mcp (depends: ecs, scheduler, renderer, input, loop, assets, scene — all build deps)
```

Build/resolve order: ecs → scheduler → renderer + input → loop + assets → context → scene → mcp.
Each plugin declares `depends: [...]` and resolves the API at runtime with `ctx.require(...)`.
There are no dependency cycles. **Cycle 2 changes:** `loop` gained a dependency on `ecs` (to publish
the `Time` resource), and the new `context` plugin depends on `ecs` + `assets`. `ecs` still depends
on nothing, so adding it as a `loop`/`context` dependency introduces no cycle.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
## 3. Framework Config & Events (verbatim public types)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

```ts
// src/config.ts
export type Config = {};   // no global config fields; all config is per-plugin

export type Events = {
  "assets:loaded": { alias: string; kind: "asset" | "bundle" };
  "scene:loaded": { name: string };
};
```

Event catalog (complete — only two events):

| Event | Payload | Emitted by | When |
|---|---|---|---|
| `assets:loaded` | `{ alias: string; kind: "asset" \| "bundle" }` | assets | once per `load()` / `loadBundle()` success; for a bundle `alias` is the bundle name |
| `scene:loaded` | `{ name: string }` | scene | after a scene's `setup` completes during `load()` (not on `unload`, not per entity) |

Design note: per-frame hot-path work (ticks, sync, render, input, structural mutations, resource
ops) is **deliberately not emitted** as kernel events — emitting an event per frame would defeat the
kernel-bypass that keeps the loop fast. ecs, scheduler, renderer, input, loop, context, and mcp emit
no events and register no hooks. The `context` plugin hands systems a typed `emit` (the framework
emitter) inside `GameContext`, but owns no event surface of its own.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
## 4. ecs — data/runtime core (Complex)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`app.ecs` returns the `World` facade **directly** — there is no wrapper layer. The plugin API IS the
World. The world is built eagerly in `createState`, so `app.ecs` is available synchronously after
`start()`. No `onStart`/`onStop` (nothing to open or tear down).

### Public types (src/plugins/ecs/types.ts)

```ts
export type Entity = number & { readonly __entity: unique symbol }; // generational handle
export type StorageStrategy = "archetype" | "sparse";

export type Component<T> = {
  readonly __id: number;
  readonly __value: T;
  (value: T): ComponentInit;     // tokens are callable: Position({x,y}) => ComponentInit
};
export type Tag = Component<Record<never, never>>;        // presence-only marker

// Opaque world-resource token — a typed singleton handle (sibling to Component<T>).
// Keyed by a stable string. __value is a phantom (type-level only) — NEVER set at runtime.
export type Resource<T> = { readonly __key: string; readonly __value?: T };

export type Stage = "input" | "update" | "physics" | "sync" | "render";
export type System = (world: World, dt: number) => void;
export type ComponentInit = { readonly component: Component<never>; readonly value: unknown };

export type Query<Values extends readonly object[]> = {
  updateEach(cb: (values: Values, entity: Entity) => void): void;  // structural ops deferred
  count(): number;
  first(): Entity | undefined;
  [Symbol.iterator](): Iterator<Entity>;
};

export type World = {
  defineComponent<T extends object>(create: () => T, opts?: { storage?: StorageStrategy }): Component<T>;
  defineTag(opts?: { storage?: StorageStrategy }): Tag;
  spawn(...parts: ComponentInit[]): Entity;
  despawn(entity: Entity): void;
  isAlive(entity: Entity): boolean;
  add<T extends object>(entity: Entity, component: Component<T>, value?: Partial<T>): void;
  remove<T extends object>(entity: Entity, component: Component<T>): void;
  has<T extends object>(entity: Entity, component: Component<T>): boolean;
  get<T extends object>(entity: Entity, component: Component<T>): T | undefined;
  set<T extends object>(entity: Entity, component: Component<T>, value: Partial<T>): void;
  query<A>(c1: Component<A>): Query<[A]>;            // overloads for arities 1..8
  // ... query(c1..c8) ...
  addSystem(stage: Stage, system: System): () => void;
  tick(dt: number): void;

  // ── Resources (typed singletons) ──
  defineResource<T>(create?: () => T): Resource<T>;             // mints "res:N" key + optional lazy factory
  setResource<T>(resource: Resource<T>, value: T): void;        // store/replace immediately
  getResource<T>(resource: Resource<T>): T | undefined;         // lazy-init from factory; undefined if unset+none
  resource<T>(resource: Resource<T>): T;                        // same, but THROWS if unset + no factory
  hasResource<T>(resource: Resource<T>): boolean;               // true if value set OR factory exists
  removeResource<T>(resource: Resource<T>): void;               // clear value; factory re-inits next read
};

export type Config = {
  initialCapacity: number;        // @default 1024
  maxStructuralOpsWarn: number;   // @default 0 (disabled)
};
export type Api = World;          // the plugin API contract IS the World facade
```

### Config

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `initialCapacity` | `number` | `1024` | Pre-allocated entity index slots. Slot table and component columns grow automatically; tuning only avoids early reallocations. |
| `maxStructuralOpsWarn` | `number` | `0` | Warn (via `ctx.log`) past this many structural ops in one flush, to catch runaway spawn storms. `0` disables. Resource ops are NOT counted. |

### Semantics & design

- **Generational entities:** an `Entity` packs a 16-bit index + 16-bit generation as
  `(generation << 16) | index`. `despawn` bumps the slot generation, so stale handles are
  detectably dead via `isAlive` (use-after-free guard). Freed indices go on a `freeList` and are
  recycled.
- **Archetype object-SoA storage:** entities sharing the same sorted component signature live in one
  archetype with parallel columns (one `entities` column + one per component). `despawn` uses
  swap-remove to keep columns dense (O(1)); `add`/`remove` migrate the entity between archetypes.
- **Storage seam:** each component is `"archetype"` (default, cache-coherent) or `"sparse"` (a
  per-component `Map<Entity, value>` — good for high-churn tags/timers). `defineTag` defaults to
  `"sparse"`. Queries return identical results across strategies.
- **Deferred command buffer:** while iterating (inside `updateEach` or any system), `spawn`/
  `despawn`/`add`/`remove` are enqueued, not applied mid-iteration. `spawn` still returns a usable
  `Entity` synchronously (index/generation reserved immediately; insertion deferred). The buffer is
  drained at each stage boundary inside `tick`. Outside iteration, ops apply immediately. This is
  the only structural-mutation path safe during iteration — and the path every `mcp` mutating tool
  uses.
- **Ticking:** `tick(dt)` runs each stage's systems in fixed order `input → update → physics → sync
  → render`, flushing the command buffer between stages. `dt` is the frame delta in seconds.

### World resources (typed singletons) — first-class systems access

World resources are typed singletons attached to the world: shared services and state that systems
reach **through the `world` argument** (`(world, dt)`), with no closure capture. Each resource is
identified by an opaque, stably-keyed `Resource<T>` token (a sibling to `Component<T>`). The system
signature is unchanged — this is non-breaking.

Internally the world owns two private maps: `resourceValues` (set values, keyed by `Resource.__key`)
and `resourceFactories` (lazy-init factories registered by `defineResource(create)`). `defineResource`
mints monotonic `"res:N"` keys. Framework well-known tokens use reserved fixed-key prefixes that
never collide: the `context` plugin's `Assets` (`"ctx:assets"`) / `GameContext` (`"ctx:game"`) and
the `loop` plugin's `Time` (`"loop:time"`).

**Resource ops are IMMEDIATE — never command-buffered.** Unlike `spawn`/`despawn`/`add`/`remove`
(which defer during iteration), all resource methods apply synchronously even inside `updateEach`
or a system, because they touch only the resource maps, never archetype layout — so they cannot
corrupt an in-flight query. The world's internal `iterating` flag is deliberately ignored by every
resource method, and resource ops are **not** counted by `maxStructuralOpsWarn`.

| Method | Signature | Semantics |
|---|---|---|
| `defineResource` | `defineResource<T>(create?: () => T): Resource<T>` | Mint a token with an auto-generated stable key (`"res:N"`). An optional `create` factory lazily initializes the value on first read (memoized thereafter); it stores **no** value at define time. Call once at module/setup scope. |
| `setResource` | `setResource<T>(resource: Resource<T>, value: T): void` | Store or replace the value. Applies immediately, even during iteration. |
| `getResource` | `getResource<T>(resource: Resource<T>): T \| undefined` | Read the value. Lazily initializes from the factory if one was registered and the value is unset (memoized); returns `undefined` if neither a value nor a factory exists. |
| `resource` | `resource<T>(resource: Resource<T>): T` | Read the value, asserting presence — same lazy-init as `getResource`, but **throws** an actionable error instead of returning `undefined` when the resource is unset and has no factory. |
| `hasResource` | `hasResource<T>(resource: Resource<T>): boolean` | `true` if a read would succeed — a value is set, **or** a default factory exists. |
| `removeResource` | `removeResource<T>(resource: Resource<T>): void` | Clear the stored value. A factory, if any, re-initializes it on the next read. Immediate, never buffered. |

`getResource` vs `resource`: both perform the same lazy-init. `getResource` returns `T | undefined`
(caller handles absence); `resource` returns `T` and **throws** when the resource is unset and no
factory was registered. The thrown error is:

```
[game] world.resource() — resource "<key>" is not set.
  Set it with world.setResource(token, value), or define it with a factory:
  world.defineResource(() => …). Framework resources (Assets, GameContext, Time) are wired at app.start().
```

```ts
// Define once at setup; an optional factory supplies the default (lazy-init + memoize).
const Score = app.ecs.defineResource(() => ({ value: 0, combo: 1 }));

app.ecs.setResource(Score, { value: 100, combo: 3 });   // immediate, anywhere

app.ecs.addSystem("update", (w) => {
  const score = w.resource(Score);     // asserts present → { value, combo }
  if (score.combo > 5) score.value *= 2;
  const maybe = w.getResource(Score);  // { value, combo } | undefined
  if (maybe) maybe.value += 10;
});
```

### Example

```ts
const Position = app.ecs.defineComponent(() => ({ x: 0, y: 0 }));
const Velocity = app.ecs.defineComponent(() => ({ dx: 0, dy: 0 }), { storage: "sparse" });
const Dead = app.ecs.defineTag();

const e = app.ecs.spawn(Position({ x: 0, y: 0 }), Velocity({ dx: 2, dy: 1 }));
app.ecs.set(e, Position, { x: 20 });
const pos = app.ecs.get(e, Position);   // { x: 20, y: 0 } | undefined

app.ecs.addSystem("update", (w, dt) => {
  w.query(Position, Velocity).updateEach(([p, v]) => { p.x += v.dx * dt; p.y += v.dy * dt; });
});
for (const entity of app.ecs.query(Dead)) app.ecs.despawn(entity);
app.ecs.tick(1 / 60);   // normally driven by loop → scheduler
```

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
## 5. scheduler — the ordered stage contract (Standard)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

A thin, typed facade over the ECS world's stage/system registry. Stores no state of its own
(`State = Record<never, never>`); the registry lives entirely in the ecs world. No lifecycle hooks.

### Public types (src/plugins/scheduler/types.ts)

```ts
export type Config = { strictStages: boolean }; // @default true
export type Api = {
  readonly stages: readonly Stage[];                          // ["input","update","physics","sync","render"]
  addSystem(stage: Stage, system: System): () => void;        // forwards to world.addSystem
  tick(dt: number): void;                                     // forwards to world.tick
};
export type { Stage, System, World } from "../ecs/types";
```

### Stages

| Order | Stage | Typical use |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `input` | Apply queued input + externally-enqueued mutations (the mcp plugin drains its pending mutations here). |
| 2 | `update` | Game logic / gameplay systems. |
| 3 | `physics` | Movement and physics integration. |
| 4 | `sync` | Reconcile simulation state into render-facing data (the renderer's Transform→Pixi sync runs here). |
| 5 | `render` | Renderer-facing systems. |

The command buffer is flushed between stages, so structural ops queued in one stage are visible to
the next.

### Config

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `strictStages` | `boolean` | `true` | `true` → `addSystem` with an unknown stage throws an `Error` listing valid stages. `false` → logs a warning via `ctx.log.warn`, registers nothing, returns a no-op unsubscribe. |

`addSystem(stage, system)` returns an unsubscribe function. The ecs world is resolved lazily inside
`addSystem`/`tick` via `ctx.require(ecsPlugin)`.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
## 6. renderer — PixiJS v8 backend (Complex)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Isolates all of PixiJS v8 behind one plugin. Creates the Pixi `Application` asynchronously in
`onStart` with `autoStart: false` (Pixi's own ticker never fires — the loop drives every frame),
registers a `sync`-stage system mirroring ECS `Transform` into Pixi display objects, and destroys
the `Application` in `onStop` to guard against Pixi v8 VRAM leaks. Pixi types never cross the plugin
boundary except `HTMLCanvasElement` (getView) and `Container` (attach/getStage).

### Public types (src/plugins/renderer/types.ts)

```ts
export type TransformValue = { x: number; y: number; rotation: number; scaleX: number; scaleY: number };

export type Config = {
  width: number;       // @default 800
  height: number;      // @default 600
  background: number;  // @default 0x000000
  resolution: number;  // @default 0 (→ window.devicePixelRatio, then 1 headless)
  antialias: boolean;  // @default true
  mount: string | undefined; // @default undefined (headless / manual)
};

export type Api = {
  readonly Transform: Component<TransformValue>;  // throws if read before app.start()
  attach(entity: Entity, view: Container): void;  // Container/Sprite/Graphics; marks dirty
  detach(entity: Entity): void;                   // destroy()s view; idempotent
  render(): void;                                 // draws one frame; no-op before start
  getView(): HTMLCanvasElement | undefined;       // canvas for manual mounting
  getStage(): Container | undefined;              // root Pixi stage
  markDirty(entity: Entity): void;                // re-sync view on next "sync" tick
};
```

### Config

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `width` | `number` | `800` | Canvas width in CSS pixels. |
| `height` | `number` | `600` | Canvas height in CSS pixels. |
| `background` | `number` | `0x000000` | Background fill color (hex, e.g. `0x1099bb`). |
| `resolution` | `number` | `0` | Device-pixel-ratio resolution; `0` falls back to `window.devicePixelRatio` (then `1` headless). |
| `antialias` | `boolean` | `true` | Enable antialiasing. |
| `mount` | `string \| undefined` | `undefined` | CSS selector to auto-mount the canvas on start. `undefined` = headless / manual via `getView()`. |

### Lifecycle & design

- **`onStart`**: `new Application()` → `await app.init({ width, height, background, antialias,
  autoStart: false, resolution })`. On reject, `app.destroy(...)` then rethrow (no half-open GPU
  context). On success: optionally append `app.canvas` to `document.querySelector(config.mount)`
  (warns if the selector matches nothing); defines the `Transform` component on the ECS world and
  stores the token; registers the sync system via `scheduler.addSystem("sync", …)`; writes `app`
  into state; stashes `{ app, views }` in a module-level `WeakMap` keyed on `ctx.global`.
- **`onStop`**: reads `{ app, views }` from the `WeakMap` (teardown ctx is only `{ global }`),
  `destroy()`s every managed `Container`, then `app.destroy(true, { children: true, texture: true,
  textureSource: true })` for full VRAM cleanup, and deletes the `WeakMap` entry.
- **Shared `Transform` token:** defined once in `onStart`, read by both the API getter and the sync
  system from state, so they can never diverge. Reading `app.renderer.Transform` before `start()`
  throws.
- **Dirty-flag sync:** `attach`/`markDirty` add the entity to `state.dirty`; each `sync` tick
  repositions only dirty views (`container.position`, `rotation`, `scale` from `Transform`) then
  clears the set. A second pass diffs `state.views` against `world.isAlive(...)` to dispose views of
  despawned entities — replacing per-despawn events with one liveness diff.
- **Headless degradation:** DOM globals are probed structurally; runs in a Node/headless runtime
  when `mount` is `undefined`. Runtime package: `pixi.js@^8`. Composes with `@moku-labs/web` for DOM
  mounting when `mount` is a selector.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
## 7. input — polled keyboard/pointer (Standard)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

A **polled** model, not event-driven — nothing about input flows through the kernel event bus. DOM
listeners mutate a live resource; once per frame an `"input"`-stage system freezes it into an
immutable `InputSnapshot` and clears the edge sets, so every later system in the tick reads a
consistent, frame-aligned view. `justPressed`/`justReleased` are true for exactly one frame.

### Public types (src/plugins/input/types.ts)

```ts
export type InputSnapshot = {
  isDown(key: string): boolean;        // held (key matches KeyboardEvent.key, e.g. "ArrowRight", "Space", "a")
  justPressed(key: string): boolean;   // one frame only; key-repeat ignored
  justReleased(key: string): boolean;  // one frame only
  readonly pointer: { readonly x: number; readonly y: number; readonly buttons: number };
};

export type Config = {
  target: string;          // @default "window" ("window" → global; else CSS selector via querySelector)
  pointer: boolean;        // @default true
  keyboard: boolean;       // @default true
  preventDefault: boolean; // @default false
};

export type Api = { snapshot(): InputSnapshot };
```

### Config

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `target` | `string` | `"window"` | DOM target for listeners. `"window"` = global window; any other string is a CSS selector resolved via `document.querySelector` at start. |
| `pointer` | `boolean` | `true` | Attach `pointermove`/`pointerdown`/`pointerup`; track position + buttons. |
| `keyboard` | `boolean` | `true` | Attach `keydown`/`keyup`; track key state. |
| `preventDefault` | `boolean` | `false` | Call `event.preventDefault()` on tracked key events (e.g. stop arrow/space scrolling). |

`pointer.buttons` is a bitmask: `1` = left, `2` = right, `4` = middle. `snapshot()` is stable for
the whole tick. Before the first tick, `snapshot()` returns an initial snapshot backed by empty
sets. Lifecycle attaches/detaches DOM listeners (`onStart`/`onStop`), tracked through a module-level
`WeakMap` keyed on `ctx.global`. Target resolution is node-safe; falls back to `globalThis`
headless. Depends only on `scheduler` (which already depends on ecs).

### Example

```ts
app.scheduler.addSystem("update", (world, dt) => {
  const input = app.input.snapshot();
  if (input.isDown("ArrowLeft")) player.x -= player.speed * dt;
  if (input.justPressed("Space")) player.jump();
  if (input.justReleased("Space")) player.endChargedJump();
  const { x, y, buttons } = input.pointer;
  if (buttons & 1) player.aimAt(x, y);
});
```

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
## 8. loop — fixed-timestep rAF loop + Time resource (Standard)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Owns the frame. The framework's **kernel-bypassing hot path**: Moku Core has no built-in per-frame
tick, so the loop drives `scheduler.tick` → `world.tick` and `renderer.render` directly, with no
event-bus dispatch on the per-frame path. It also publishes the **`Time`** ECS world resource — the
canonical frame clock — and is its sole writer.

### Public types (src/plugins/loop/types.ts)

```ts
import type { Resource } from "../ecs/types";

// Frame clock published as the Time world resource. Consumer-facing fields are readonly;
// the loop mutates the backing object in place each fixed step — no per-step allocation.
export type TimeState = {
  readonly dt: number;       // fixed step length, in SECONDS (always == config.fixedDt)
  readonly elapsed: number;  // total simulated time since start, in SECONDS (sum of all fixed steps)
  readonly frame: number;    // count of fixed steps simulated since start (a count, not seconds)
};

export type Config = {
  fixedDt: number;          // @default 1/60
  maxFrameDelta: number;    // @default 0.25
  maxStepsPerFrame: number; // @default 5
  autoStart: boolean;       // @default true
};

export type Api = {
  start(): void;        // begin/resume rAF loop (no-op if running); resets accumulator + lastTime
  stop(): void;         // pause; cancelAnimationFrame, running = false (no-op if not running)
  isRunning(): boolean;
  step(): void;         // exactly one fixed tick + render, NO real-time accumulation; also advances Time
  readonly time: Resource<TimeState>;  // well-known Time token; also exported as `Time` from loop/resources
};
```

### Fixed-timestep model

Each rAF frame: (1) compute real delta in seconds (first frame only seeds `lastTime`, skips
ticking); (2) clamp delta to `maxFrameDelta` (spiral-of-death guard); (3) add to `accumulator`, run
`scheduler.tick(fixedDt)` while `accumulator >= fixedDt`, up to `maxStepsPerFrame` times (each step
subtracts `fixedDt`; leftover carries forward); (4) `renderer.render()` once; (5) re-schedule via
`requestAnimationFrame` unless `stop()` cleared `running`. A `visibilitychange` listener resets
`accumulator` + `lastTime` on tab return so a backgrounded tab does not produce a catch-up burst.

### The `Time` world resource

The loop is the **sole writer of frame time**, so it publishes a first-class `Time` ECS world
resource. ECS systems have signature `(world, dt)`; the `dt` argument is the fixed step, but a system
that needs the running total or frame count reads them from `Time` via `world.resource(Time)` rather
than threading extra arguments. `Time` lives in the loop (not `context`) so a single owner avoids two
plugins racing on one clock object.

- **Binding:** on `onStart` the loop calls `world.setResource(Time, { dt: 0, elapsed: 0, frame: 0 })`
  and caches that same object on its per-instance `LoopRuntime`. Each fixed step it mutates the cached
  object **in place** immediately before `scheduler.tick(fixedDt)` — so a system running that step
  already sees the updated clock. Because the world's registry holds the same reference,
  `world.resource(Time)` reflects every update with **no per-frame allocation** and no repeated
  `setResource`.
- **Per-step update** (in both the rAF callback and `step()`):

```ts
runtime.time.dt = config.fixedDt;       // current step length (seconds)
runtime.time.elapsed += config.fixedDt; // running total (seconds)
runtime.time.frame += 1;                // step counter
```

- **Two ways to reach the token** (both the same `Resource<TimeState>`):
  `import { Time } from "../loop/resources"` (module-level const, `__key: "loop:time"`), or
  `app.loop.time` (surfaced on the public API for consumer code).
- **Teardown:** `Time` needs no explicit teardown — its value is released with the ECS world on stop.

```ts
import { Time } from "../loop/resources";
import type { World } from "../ecs/types";

app.scheduler.addSystem("update", (world: World, dt: number) => {
  const t = world.resource(Time);   // → { dt, elapsed, frame }
  // t.dt === the dt argument === config.fixedDt (seconds)
  if (t.frame % 60 === 0) console.log(`elapsed ${t.elapsed.toFixed(2)}s (frame ${t.frame})`);
});

const clock = world.resource(app.loop.time);  // → e.g. { dt: 0.016, elapsed: 1.23, frame: 74 }
```

### Config

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `fixedDt` | `number` | `1 / 60` | Fixed simulation step in seconds; each `scheduler.tick` receives this. |
| `maxFrameDelta` | `number` | `0.25` | Max real delta (s) consumed per frame before clamping. |
| `maxStepsPerFrame` | `number` | `5` | Hard cap on fixed steps per frame after clamping. |
| `autoStart` | `boolean` | `true` | Schedule the first frame in `onStart`; `false` → call `app.loop.start()`. |

`start`/`stop` double as resume/pause (mcp `loop:resume`/`loop:pause`). `step()` is what the mcp
`loop:step` tool drives — deterministic, works whether running or stopped, and advances `Time`
exactly as a real frame would. Lifecycle: rAF id + `visibilitychange` listener live in a
module-level `WeakMap` keyed on `ctx.global`; `onStart` also binds the `Time` resource; `onStop`
cancels the pending rAF and removes the listener (prevents a zombie rAF). Headless: rAF/`document`
read structurally; scheduling is a no-op while `step()` still works. **Dependencies: scheduler,
renderer, ecs** — `ecs` is the Cycle 2 addition (needed to call `world.setResource(Time, …)`); no
cycle, since `ecs` depends on nothing. No package dependencies beyond `@moku-labs/core`.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
## 9. assets — Pixi v8 Assets wrapper (Standard)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Loads and caches textures/spritesheets by alias, builds `Sprite`s, emits `assets:loaded`. The
renderer must boot first (`depends: [rendererPlugin]`); each method lazily calls
`ctx.require(rendererPlugin)` to guarantee the Pixi `Application` (and shared Assets/GPU context) is
running. No `onStart`/`onStop` — it borrows the renderer's Application rather than owning a resource.

### Public types (src/plugins/assets/types.ts)

```ts
export type Events = { "assets:loaded": { alias: string; kind: "asset" | "bundle" } };

export type Config = {
  basePath: string;                          // @default ""
  manifest: Readonly<Record<string, string>>; // @default {}
  throwOnError: boolean;                     // @default true
};

export type Api = {
  load(alias: string): Promise<Texture>;
  loadBundle(bundle: string, entries: Readonly<Record<string, string>>): Promise<Record<string, Texture>>;
  get(alias: string): Texture | undefined;   // cache read; does NOT load
  sprite(alias: string): Promise<Sprite>;    // load-on-miss, then build Sprite
  isLoaded(alias: string): boolean;          // session state.loaded, not the Pixi cache
};
```

### Config

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `basePath` | `string` | `""` | Base URL/path prepended to every resolved URL. |
| `manifest` | `Readonly<Record<string, string>>` | `{}` | alias → URL; aliases here are preferred over the raw alias string. |
| `throwOnError` | `boolean` | `true` | `true` → rethrow load errors. `false` → log via `ctx.log.error` and `load()` resolves `undefined` (typed `Texture` for ergonomics — do not rely on the value). |

### URL resolution

For each alias: if `manifest[alias]` exists → use it; else → treat the alias as the URL. `basePath`
is prepended when non-empty. E.g. with `basePath: "assets/"`, `manifest: { ship: "sprites/ship.png"
}`: `load("ship")` → `Assets.load("assets/sprites/ship.png")`; `load("tank.png")` →
`Assets.load("assets/tank.png")`.

`assets:loaded` is coarse — emitted once per `load()`/`loadBundle()` call, never per texture. A
bundle of N textures emits exactly one event keyed on the bundle name. `sprite()` checks `get(alias)`
first and only loads on a cache miss (a repeat call does not re-emit).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
## 10. context — well-known world resources (Standard)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

ECS systems have signature `(world, dt)` — they never see the plugin `ctx`. The `context` plugin
closes that gap: at framework start it binds two **well-known world resources** onto the ECS world,
so any system can reach framework services via `world.resource(token)` with no closure over `app`.
It owns no per-frame logic and stores no domain data (`State = Record<never, never>`) — it is a thin
wiring + facade plugin sitting **above** both `ecs` and `assets`.

1. **`Assets`** — the `assets` plugin API (`load`/`loadBundle`/`get`/`sprite`/`isLoaded`). **Always**
   bound, regardless of config.
2. **`GameContext`** — a curated, hot-path-safe facade `{ log, emit, env }` built from this plugin's
   own `ctx`. Bound only when `config.bindGameContext` is `true`.

### Public types (src/plugins/context/types.ts, resources.ts)

```ts
import type { EnvApi, LogApi } from "@moku-labs/common";
import type { EmitFn } from "@moku-labs/core";
import type { Events as FrameworkEvents } from "../../config";
import type { Api as AssetsApi } from "../assets/types";
import type { Resource } from "../ecs/types";

export type Config = {
  bindGameContext: boolean;   // @default true
};

export type State = Record<never, never>;  // no per-instance state; values live in the ecs world

// Curated, hot-path-safe game context exposed to systems via the GameContext resource.
export type GameContextValue = {
  readonly log: LogApi;                      // ctx.log from the common logPlugin
  readonly emit: EmitFn<FrameworkEvents>;    // emit a coarse framework event (assets:loaded | scene:loaded)
  readonly env: EnvApi;                      // ctx.env from the common envPlugin
};

// API — the well-known resource tokens (static; valid before start).
export type Api = {
  readonly assets: Resource<AssetsApi>;       // token; read in a system: world.resource(assets)
  readonly game: Resource<GameContextValue>;  // token; read in a system: world.resource(game)
};

// resources.ts — fixed-key module consts:
export const Assets: Resource<AssetsApi> = { __key: "ctx:assets" };
export const GameContext: Resource<GameContextValue> = { __key: "ctx:game" };
```

### Config

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `bindGameContext` | `boolean` | `true` | When `true`, bind the curated `GameContext` resource (`{ log, emit, env }`) at start. `Assets` is bound either way. Disable only if a consumer wants to provide its own game-context resource. |

### Lifecycle & design

- **`onStart`-only:** the single managed concern is the world-resource bindings, set up once before
  the first frame. `start(ctx)` does three things: (1) `const world = ctx.require(ecsPlugin)`;
  (2) `world.setResource(Assets, ctx.require(assetsPlugin))` — **always**; (3) if
  `ctx.config.bindGameContext`, `world.setResource(GameContext, { log: ctx.log, emit: ctx.emit, env: ctx.env })`.
  No `onInit` (binding needs the started `assets` API + world, resolved by `ctx.require` at start)
  and no `onStop` (the bindings are released with the world). The `onStart` reference carries a
  `// @no-resource-check` — the world-level binding itself satisfies the "no lifecycle without a
  resource" rule, mirroring how `renderer.onStart` mints the shared `Transform` token.
- **Static API, fixed-key tokens:** `createApi` returns the two token consts (`{ assets: Assets,
  game: GameContext }`). The tokens are plain module consts (fixed keys `"ctx:assets"` /
  `"ctx:game"`), valid to reference/capture immediately — only their *values* are unset until
  `start`. This differs from `world.defineResource`, which mints auto-keyed (`"res:N"`) tokens.
  `app.context.assets` is the same object as the exported `Assets`; `app.context.game` is the same
  as `GameContext`.
- **Curated `GameContext`:** exactly `{ log, emit, env }` — `log`/`env` are the *same references* as
  `ctx.log`/`ctx.env`; `emit` forwards `ctx.emit`. It deliberately **excludes** kernel escape
  hatches (`require`/`has`/`global`) so systems can't re-enter the kernel mid-frame. Frame time is
  delivered separately as the loop's `Time` resource, not here.
- **`depends: [ecs, assets]` only:** the global `Events` declared in `createCoreConfig` are visible
  to every plugin's `ctx.emit`, so `scene:loaded` is emittable through `GameContext.emit` without
  depending on `scene` — avoiding a runtime-unused dependency.
- **Ordering:** `context.onStart` runs after `assets.onStart` (start runs in dependency order), and
  the loop's first tick fires on the next animation frame — after `app.start()` has awaited every
  `onStart` — so `Assets`/`GameContext` are always bound before any system runs.

### Example

```ts
const { assets, game } = app.context;   // capture the well-known tokens once (like component tokens)

app.ecs.addSystem("render", (world, _dt) => {
  const a = world.resource(assets);   // AssetsApi — first-class, no `app` capture
  const g = world.resource(game);     // { log, emit, env }
  const ship = a.get("ship");
  if (!ship) g.log.warn("ship texture not loaded");
});
```

`world.resource(token)` throws the ecs "resource is not set" error if read **before** `start` (the
tokens are valid immediately, but their values are unset until `onStart` binds them). With
`bindGameContext: false`, `Assets` is still bound but reading `game` throws.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
## 11. scene — named scene lifecycle + ownership (Standard)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

A scene is a registered `setup(world)` that spawns the entities for one screen/level. The crux is
**entity-ownership tracking**: during `setup`, the plugin hands a thin tracking wrapper around the
world whose `spawn` is intercepted — every spawned entity is recorded as owned. Transitioning (or
`unload`) detaches each owned entity's renderer view and despawns it, so nothing leaks between
scenes. If a scene declares a `bundle`, it is pre-loaded via the assets plugin before `setup` runs.
(The tracking wrapper delegates the world resource methods through to the real world, so its public
API is unchanged.)

### Public types (src/plugins/scene/types.ts)

```ts
export type Events = { "scene:loaded": { name: string } };

export type SceneDefinition = {
  setup: (world: World) => void | Promise<void>;  // spawns entities; may be async
  bundle?: Readonly<Record<string, string>>;       // optional alias→url, pre-loaded before setup
};

export type Config = {
  initial: string | undefined; // @default undefined (RESERVED — not auto-loaded in this version)
  despawnOnUnload: boolean;    // @default true
};

export type Api = {
  define(name: string, definition: SceneDefinition): void;  // re-defining overwrites
  load(name: string): Promise<void>;                        // throws if name was never defined
  unload(): void;                                            // no-op if no scene; does NOT emit scene:loaded
  currentScene(): string | undefined;
};
```

### Config

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `initial` | `string \| undefined` | `undefined` | Reserved; NOT auto-loaded in this version — call `app.scene.load(...)` after `app.start()`. |
| `despawnOnUnload` | `boolean` | `true` | `true` → owned entities detached + despawned on unload. `false` → ownership cleared and `current` reset, entities left alive. |

### `load(name)` ordered steps

1. **Unload current** — despawn the previous scene's owned entities + detach renderer views (subject
   to `despawnOnUnload`), reset `current`.
2. **Pre-load bundle** — if `definition.bundle` is set, `await assets.loadBundle(name, bundle)`
   (keyed by scene name).
3. **Run setup** — `await definition.setup(wrappedWorld)`; every `spawn` is tracked as owned.
4. **Commit + emit** — `current = name`, emit `scene:loaded` with `{ name }`.

Partial-spawn safety: entities are recorded as each `spawn` returns, so a `setup` that throws
part-way still has its spawned entities cleaned up on the next transition. Depends on ecs (world +
`despawn`), renderer (`detach`), assets (`loadBundle`). Lazy `ctx.require` inside methods.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
## 12. mcp — first-class MCP server (Complex)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Attaches a Model Context Protocol server to the running framework. Agent clients (Claude, Cursor,
etc.) query live state, control the loop, spawn/despawn entities, trigger scene loads, and capture
screenshots — without touching game code. Depends on all eight other plugins.

### Public types (src/plugins/mcp/types.ts)

```ts
export type Config = {
  transports: ReadonlyArray<"stdio" | "http">; // @default ["stdio"]
  httpHost: string;                             // @default "127.0.0.1"
  httpPort: number;                             // @default 3333
  httpAuth: "none" | "bearer";                  // @default "none"
  bearerToken: string;                          // @default ""  (required + non-empty when httpAuth==="bearer")
  enableMutations: boolean;                     // @default true
};

export type Api = {
  isRunning(): boolean;                  // true when connected on ≥1 transport
  httpEndpoint(): string | undefined;    // e.g. "http://127.0.0.1:3333/mcp", or undefined (stdio-only)
  toolNames(): readonly string[];        // names of all registered tools (stable after onStart)
};
```

### Config

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `transports` | `ReadonlyArray<"stdio" \| "http">` | `["stdio"]` | Which transports to expose. |
| `httpHost` | `string` | `"127.0.0.1"` | HTTP bind address (localhost only by default). |
| `httpPort` | `number` | `3333` | HTTP server port. |
| `httpAuth` | `"none" \| "bearer"` | `"none"` | `"none"` trusts the local network; `"bearer"` requires a token on every request. |
| `bearerToken` | `string` | `""` | Required (non-empty, validated at startup) when `httpAuth === "bearer"`. |
| `enableMutations` | `boolean` | `true` | `false` → only the three read-only tools are registered. |

### MCP tool catalog

Read-only (always registered):

| Tool | Description |
|---|---|
| `ecs:query` | MCP-tracked entity ids and count. v1: `componentNames` filter not applied (tokens are opaque). |
| `renderer:screenshot` | Current frame as a raw base64 PNG string (data-URI prefix stripped). Error result if the renderer hasn't started. |
| `scene:getInfo` | Current scene name, or `undefined`. |

Mutating (registered when `enableMutations: true`):

| Tool | Description |
|---|---|
| `ecs:spawn` | Spawn an entity with no components. Returns `{ entity: number }`; adds it to the MCP-tracked set. |
| `ecs:despawn` | Despawn an entity by numeric id; removes it from the tracked set. |
| `ecs:setComponent` | v1 no-op (tokens not addressable by string name). Returns `{ status: "v1-noop" }`. |
| `ecs:removeComponent` | v1 no-op. Returns `{ status: "v1-noop" }`. |
| `loop:step` | Advance the loop one fixed step (deterministic). |
| `loop:pause` | Pause the rAF loop. |
| `loop:resume` | Resume the rAF loop. |
| `scene:load` | Load a named scene (enqueued; unloads current first). |
| `game:reset` | Despawn all tracked entities, clear the set, call `scene.unload()`. Deferred to the next input-stage tick. |

### MCP resource catalog

| URI | Description |
|---|---|
| `game://world/snapshot` | JSON of all MCP-tracked entity ids. v1: only entities spawned via `ecs:spawn`. |
| `game://systems/list` | JSON list of registered scheduler stage names. |
| `game://stats/frame` | JSON with current `frame`, `lastDt` (s), MCP-tracked `entityCount`. |
| `game://scene/current` | JSON with the `current` scene name, or `undefined`. |

### Design notes

- **Frame-safe mutations:** mutating tools push closures into a `pending: Array<() => void>` queue
  drained on each `"input"` stage tick by a registered ECS system. Read-only tools and loop controls
  run directly between frames.
- **Tracked entities:** only entities spawned through `ecs:spawn` are tracked (the world has no
  public enumerate-all API in v1).
- **SDK isolation:** all `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` imports are confined to `transport.ts`.
- **Tool naming:** tools use `domain:action` (`ecs:spawn`, `loop:step`). The MCP SDK warns `:` is
  non-standard (SEP-986 permits `A–Z a–z 0–9 _ - .`); registration still proceeds, but strict
  clients may reject/mis-display — front them with a name-mapping adapter if needed.
- **Bearer auth:** when `httpAuth: "bearer"`, the HTTP handler compares the `Authorization` header
  in constant time (`node:crypto` `timingSafeEqual`).
- **WeakMap pattern:** per-instance `McpHandle` lives in a module-level `WeakMap` keyed on
  `ctx.global`, mirroring the loop plugin.

Runtime packages: `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk`, `hono` (HTTP transport), `zod` (tool input schemas).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
## 13. End-to-end example (scene → spawn → resources → loop → MCP)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

```ts
import { createApp } from "game";
import { Container } from "pixi.js";
import { Time } from "game"; // or "../loop/resources" — the well-known Time token

const app = createApp({
  pluginConfigs: {
    renderer: { width: 1280, height: 720, background: 0x1099bb, mount: "#game" },
    loop:     { fixedDt: 1 / 60, maxStepsPerFrame: 5, autoStart: true },
    assets:   { basePath: "assets/", manifest: { hero: "sprites/hero.png" } },
    context:  { bindGameContext: true },
    mcp:      { transports: ["stdio", "http"], httpPort: 3333, enableMutations: true }
  }
});

await app.start();   // boots Pixi Application, attaches input listeners, binds resources, connects MCP

// Components.
const Velocity = app.ecs.defineComponent(() => ({ dx: 0, dy: 0 }));

// Capture the well-known resource tokens once.
const { assets, game } = app.context;

// A scene that pre-loads a bundle then spawns a transformed, rendered entity.
app.scene.define("level1", {
  bundle: { hero: "sprites/hero.png" },
  setup: async (world) => {
    const hero = world.spawn(                                  // tracked as owned by "level1"
      app.renderer.Transform({ x: 200, y: 200, rotation: 0, scaleX: 1, scaleY: 1 }),
      Velocity({ dx: 40, dy: 0 })
    );
    const sprite = await app.assets.sprite("hero");           // texture already cached by the bundle
    app.renderer.attach(hero, sprite);
  }
});

// Movement system (update stage); reaches services through `world`, not `app`.
app.scheduler.addSystem("update", (world, dt) => {
  const a = world.resource(assets);                           // AssetsApi — no closure over `app`
  const t = world.resource(app.loop.time);                   // { dt, elapsed, frame }
  void a; void t;
  world.query(app.renderer.Transform, Velocity).updateEach(([transform, v], entity) => {
    transform.x += v.dx * dt;
    app.renderer.markDirty(entity);                           // re-sync the view next "sync" tick
  });
});

await app.scene.load("level1");   // emits scene:loaded { name: "level1" }; loop is already driving frames

// The MCP server is live: an agent can now hit app.mcp.httpEndpoint() to
// loop:pause / loop:step / ecs:spawn / scene:load / renderer:screenshot, etc.
console.log(app.mcp.isRunning(), app.mcp.httpEndpoint(), app.mcp.toolNames());

await app.stop();   // destroys the Pixi Application (frees VRAM), removes listeners, closes MCP
```

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
## 14. Error handling & gotchas
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

- `createApp(...)` is synchronous; `app.start()` / `app.stop()` are async and must be awaited.
- `app.renderer.Transform` throws if read **before** `app.start()` — the token is defined in
  `onStart`. Spawn renderable entities only after start (e.g. in a scene `setup`, or after `await
  app.start()`).
- `world.resource(token)` **throws** an actionable error when the resource is unset and has no
  factory; `world.getResource(token)` returns `undefined` instead. Framework resources (`Assets`,
  `GameContext`, `Time`) are unset until `app.start()` binds them — reading them before start throws.
  With `context: { bindGameContext: false }`, `Assets` is still bound but reading `GameContext`
  throws. Resource ops are immediate (never command-buffered) and not counted by
  `maxStructuralOpsWarn`.
- `app.scheduler.addSystem(stage, …)` with an unknown stage throws by default (`strictStages: true`);
  set `false` to downgrade to a logged warning + no-op.
- `app.assets.load(...)` rejects on failure by default; set `throwOnError: false` to log and resolve
  `undefined` instead (still typed `Texture` — do not rely on the value).
- `app.scene.load(name)` throws if `name` was never `define`d.
- `mcp` with `httpAuth: "bearer"` validates `bearerToken` as non-empty at startup; an empty token
  fails fast.
- Headless runtime: renderer (`mount: undefined`), input, and loop degrade gracefully when DOM/rAF
  globals are absent — the renderer's `render()` is a safe no-op before start, loop scheduling is a
  no-op while `step()` still ticks deterministically (and still advances `Time`).
- Structural mutations during iteration (`updateEach` / inside a system) are deferred to the command
  buffer and applied at stage boundaries; outside iteration they apply immediately.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
## 15. Scripts, requirements, layout
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Scripts (package.json, run with `bun`):

```
bun run build              # tsdown
bun run lint               # biome check . && eslint .
bun run lint:fix           # biome check --write . && eslint --fix .
bun run format             # biome format --write .
bun run test               # vitest run
bun run test:unit          # vitest run --project unit
bun run test:integration   # vitest run --project integration
bun run test:coverage      # vitest run --project unit --project integration --coverage
```

Requirements: Node `>= 24`, Bun `>= 1.3.14` (bun exclusively). TypeScript strict +
`exactOptionalPropertyTypes` + `noUncheckedIndexedAccess`. Dependencies: `@moku-labs/core`,
`@moku-labs/common`, `pixi.js@^8`; mcp adds `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk`, `hono`, `zod`. Composable
with `@moku-labs/web`.

Layout:

```
src/config.ts              # Layer 1 — createCoreConfig, Config = {}, Events, createPlugin/createCore
src/index.ts               # Layer 2 — createCore(9 plugins); exports createApp/createPlugin
src/plugins/index.ts       # plugin barrel (instances + Ecs/Scheduler/Context/... type namespaces)
src/plugins/<name>/        # api.ts, types.ts, index.ts, lifecycle.ts / resources.ts (where applicable), README.md, __tests__/
tests/                     # framework-level unit + integration tests
.planning/specs/           # design specs (intent)
```

License: MIT © moku-labs.
